title: Star Wars Missions 20: Total Destruction
content:
{{Top|rwm|reb|italics=1}} {{Book |image=[[File:Missions20.jpg]] |title=''Star Wars Missions 20:
Total Destruction'' |author=[[Ryder Windham]] |cover artist= |illustrator= |editor= |publisher=[[Scholastic]] |early release= |release date=[[April]] [[1999]]''[[Star Wars Missions 20: Total Destruction]]'' |intl release= |rereleased= |media type=Paperback |pages=77 |isbn=0590847775 |isbn2= |isbn3= |timeline=[[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]]''[[Star Wars Missions 17: Darth Vader's Return]]'' and the next three ''Star Wars Missions'' books tell a continuous story that starts with Darth Vader returning to [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] after the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]]. Though ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' states that Vader did not return to Coruscant until he had discovered Luke Skywalker's identity, [[Empire 14|''Empire'' 14]] depicts Vader returning immediately to Coruscant after crashing on Vaal, a fact which is repeated in ''[[Star Wars: The Ultimate Visual Guide: Updated and Expanded]]''. |series=''[[Star Wars Missions]]'' |preceded by=''[[Star Wars Missions 19: Bounty on Bonadan]]'' |followed by=''[[Rebel Force: Target]]'' }} '''''Star Wars Missions 20: Total Destruction''''' is the twentieth and final adventure book in the ''[[Star Wars Missions]]'' young-reader series and [[roleplaying game]]. It was published by [[Scholastic]] in [[April]] [[1999]]. ==Publisher's summary== ''You are a Rebel. On a distant planet in the Unknown Regions, the Empire is setting up a device that will allow them to control all hyperspace travel. If they succeed, the Rebellion will be crushed – and the Empire will dominate the galaxy forevermore. You are the Rebellion's only hope. Your mission: to destroy the Imperial device – and thwart Darth Vader's sinister plans.'' ''May the Force be with you.'' ==Plot summary== Learning that the ''[[Stellar Manx]]'' has been taken by Imperial forces, [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] hijacks a [[Corporate Sector Authority/Legends|Corporate Sector Authority]] [[Marauder-class Assault Corvette/Legends|''Marauder''-class corvette]], and pursues it. Meanwhile, in the hold of the ''Manx'', [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]], [[Q-7N]] and [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]] are holed up in the [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']]. Upon reaching [[Malagarr/Legends|Malagarr]], [[Frap Radicon]] explains to [[Tix]] and [[Mordak]] the concept of a [[Gravity well projector/Legends|gravity well projector]]. Concerned that Radicon is insane, the two Imperials decide to defect, and resolve to steal the ''Falcon''. They encounter Q-7N, who warns them not to upset Chewbacca. The [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiee]], along with the turncoat Imperials, escape the planet, and meet with Solo's Marauder. Solo questions the Imperials, and is convinced they can be of service. Two of the Rebels [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] [[IRD-A starfighter|IRD-As]] down to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]], evading Imperial space [[Mining/Legends|mines]]. Q-7N stows away on Scout One's craft, which subsequently makes a crash landing. Scout Two also crashes, but Scout One saves them. Scout One then disables the [[Gravity/Legends|gravity]] well projector, and escapes. Q-7N explains that Malagarr is highly dangerous to organic beings, and that the planet must be destroyed. With only Q-7N having the ability to destroy the planet, Scout One and Scout Two quickly flee the planet in the ''Manx'', while the small black [[Droid/Legends|droid]] destroys Malagarr.
